era_context: One year before 900 BCE; same era context applies. The Neo-Assyrian Empire under Ashurnasirpal II is consolidating power. Greece is in the early Archaic period. China's Western Zhou is in gradual decline. Mesoamerica is in the Maya Classic period, approaching the collapse of the southern lowlands.

events.title: Ashurnasirpal II's continued military consolidation
events.description: Ashurnasirpal II continues his systematic military campaigns and administrative reorganization. This year falls within his reign (883–859 BCE) but no specific event is individually documented for 901 BCE. The broader context is one of Assyrian expansion and the establishment of the empire's military-administrative apparatus.

sources.name: Ashurnasirpal II's annals and inscriptions (covering his entire reign)

events.certainty_note: No specific event is documented for 901 BCE; this entry reflects the general context of Ashurnasirpal II's reign.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are documented for 901 BCE in surviving sources. The entry above is a placeholder reflecting the broader era context rather than a documented historical event. This is honest documentation of a gap in the historical record.
historiographic_note: 901 BCE is not individually documented in surviving sources for any region. The historical record for this year is essentially empty. This reflects the general sparseness of year-by-year documentation in the ancient world; most surviving sources provide narrative accounts of reigns, dynasties, or longer periods rather than annual records.
